image_title: Study of the Scribe Jean Miélot
image_caption: A meticulous, photorealistic reconstruction of a master scribe's study, c. 1460.
image_alt: A photorealistic portrait of Jean Miélot, a 15th-century scribe and author, meticulously writing on a large parchment within his richly detailed scriptorium. The unique triangular spinning desk and surrounding wooden shelves, filled with hundreds of authentic vellum and leather books, replicate the complex layout of Tavernier's 1460 grisaille illumination. Specific objects, like the inkwells and the open box with two metal birds, are preserved. The entire scene is in a highly detailed, monochrome sepia/grisaille palette, simulating aged vellum. Cinematic side-lighting highlights vellum texture, wood grain, and dust motes. No modern colors. 8k.
